judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports fully satisfy all three expectations with correct root causes, fixes, and mechanisms; they are comparable.

report_1.evidence: The application connects to Postgres as a role that Postgres exempts from row-level security. `cedarline/.env` (and `.env.example`) set `DATABASE_URL=postgresql://cedar_owner:ownerpw@localhost:5432/cedar`... it is also the **owner** of the `clients` and `appointments` tables. Postgres skips RLS policies for superusers entirely, and skips them for table owners unless the table has `FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY` — which `db/migrations/0001_init.sql` does not set

report_2.evidence: `cedarline/.env` (and `.env.example`) set `DATABASE_URL=postgresql://cedar_owner:ownerpw@localhost:5432/cedar`... Postgres unconditionally skips RLS for superusers, and skips it for table owners too unless `FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY` is set (the migration only uses `ENABLE`). So every policy evaluates to "no restriction" with no error or warning

report_1.evidence: change `DATABASE_URL` in `.env`/`.env.example` to `postgresql://cedar_app:apppw@localhost:5432/cedar`... Going forward: reserve `cedar_owner` strictly for migrations (`scripts/migrate.sh`) and run all application traffic through `cedar_app`

report_2.evidence: change `DATABASE_URL` in `.env`/`.env.example` to `postgresql://cedar_app:apppw@localhost:5432/cedar`... keep two strictly separated roles: `cedar_owner` used **only** by `scripts/migrate.sh`... and `cedar_app` for everything the running app does

report_1.evidence: The role creation lives solely in `db/bootstrap/create_app_role.sql`, which `README.md` and `docs/local-setup.md` describe as a one-time **manual** first-setup step, and which the reset script never invokes... after a reset, `cedar_app` simply doesn't exist... To make it work again, the bootstrap must be re-run against the fresh cluster

report_2.evidence: `cedar_app` is created only by a manual, one-time bootstrap — `db/bootstrap/create_app_role.sql`... `scripts/reset-local-db.sh` runs `docker compose down -v`, which deletes the `cedar_pgdata` volume and with it the entire cluster including the `cedar_app` role, then brings the DB back up and re-runs only `scripts/migrate.sh`... To make it work again the teammate must re-run the bootstrap
